w:r.w:t: Every file, every change, every migration — nothing skipped.

SafePath today is a single-tenant consumer app — one user, their scenarios, their certificate. The enterprise pivot adds a multi-tenant layer on top of the existing engine without rewriting it. Your five game modes, ScenarioCore, scoring engine, AI coach, and certificate system all stay as-is. What gets added sits around them.
Core principle: the enterprise layer is a wrapper, not a replacement. All existing user-facing features continue working. New enterprise features are additive.
MANAGER and ORG_ADMIN roles in UserRole enum
This is the foundation. All other changes depend on these models being in place first. Run migrations after every schema block below.
MANAGER // department/team manager — sees team analytics
ORG_ADMIN // company-level admin — manages whole org
1.7 Add OrgScenarioPackage (which scenario packs an org licensed)
packageId String // matches key in lib/enterprise/packages.ts
csvUrl String? // stored in /public or S3
1.9 Extend User model with orgId shortcut
After all schema changes: run npm run prisma:migrate && npm run prisma:generate

Your existing lib/scenarios.ts exports a flat Record<string, Scenario>. Enterprise adds a package layer on top — a curated list of scenario IDs grouped by industry and training topic. Scenarios themselves remain in lib/scenarios.ts.
This file defines which scenarios belong to which industry package. It references existing scenario IDs from lib/scenarios.ts AND new ones you add.
industry: string // matches Industry enum value
description: 'POSH-compliant harassment awareness for IT teams.',
description: 'Machine malfunction, chemical hazards, evacuation drills.',
description: 'Emergency triage, infection control, ethical dilemmas.',
description: 'Phishing, insider threats, KYC fraud, RBI compliance.',
Use the same createMiniScenario() pattern already in your codebase. Here is one full example for manufacturing — replicate this for each package:
// In lib/scenarios.ts — add to the SCENARIOS object:
context: 'You are operating a lathe machine. An unusual vibration starts.',
prompt: 'The machine begins vibrating violently. A warning light flashes red.',
saferChoice: 'Immediately press the emergency stop and inform your supervisor.',
assertiveChoice: 'Stop the machine, step back, alert nearby workers.',
riskyChoice: 'Continue working — it might stop on its own.',
tip: 'Any abnormal machine behaviour is a stop-work signal. Never continue.',
Add one createMiniScenario() block per scenarioId listed in each ENTERPRISE_PACKAGES entry. Aim for 4-6 scenarios per package to start.

Your existing chatbot.ts hardcodes a women's safety system prompt. For enterprise, the system prompt must adapt to the organisation's industry and the employee's job role. The change is surgical — one new function, one modified call.
CORPORATE_IT: `You are a corporate compliance coach for IT professionals.
Focus on POSH Act, workplace ethics, and professional conduct.`,
MANUFACTURING: `You are a factory safety coach.
Focus on machine safety, PPE, emergency procedures, chemical hazards.
HEALTHCARE: `You are a clinical training coach.
Focus on patient safety, infection control, medical ethics, triage.`,
BANKING_FINANCE: `You are a financial compliance coach.
Focus on fraud detection, phishing, RBI regulations, insider threats.`,
'You are a workplace safety and compliance coach.'
Never victim-blame. Be empowering and practical.`
The feedback route currently builds a generic prompt. Add an optional industry context:
// In app/api/coach/feedback/route.ts, modify the POST handler:
// industry, jobRole, complianceFramework are optional — sent by enterprise clients
In app/scenario/[id]/ScenarioCore.tsx, the handleChoice function calls /api/coach/feedback. Pass enterprise context if available:
// In handleChoice(), add to the fetch body:
Managers need to see team progress without seeing individual user PII. Build this as a new route group parallel to /admin/*.
Org-level analytics — completion rates, avg scores
Member list with training status badges
Shared org shell — sidebar nav, auth guard
GET members, POST invite, DELETE remove
POST generate report, GET list reports
Every org page must verify the session user is an OrgMember with MANAGER or ORG_ADMIN orgRole:
export default async function OrgLayout({ children, params }) {
This is the data engine for the manager dashboard. It aggregates ScenarioRun and RolePlaySession data for all OrgMembers:
// 1. Get all userIds in this org
select: { userId: true, jobRole: true, dept: { select: { name: true } } }
finalConfidence: true, finalEq: true, finalRisk: true }
Companies need audit-ready records. Generate a CSV report of all employee training completions for a time period, downloadable from the manager dashboard.
import { stringify } from 'csv-stringify/sync' // npm install csv-stringify
include: { user: { select: { name: true, email: true } },
Create public/reports/ directory and add it to .gitignore. For production, replace writeFile with an S3/R2 upload using the AWS SDK or @cloudflare/workers-types.
Employees don't sign up themselves — managers invite them. Invitation = email with a special token that auto-joins them to the org on first login.
// Sends invitation email with join link
const { email, deptId, jobRole } = await req.json()
// Reads ?token= from URL, validates, creates/links user to org
// If user not registered: redirects to /register?invite=TOKEN
// If user registered: calls POST /api/join with token to create OrgMember
// After join: redirects to /dashboard with org context
When an employee logs in through an org context, their job role and department risk profile determine which scenarios are recommended first and how the AI coach frames feedback.
// Filter packages for this employee's org
// Prioritise scenarios matching job role keywords
include: { org: { include: { packages: true } }, dept: true }
This is a server component that fetches from /api/org/[orgId]/analytics and renders the org-level overview. Key metrics to show:
mean finalConfidence across all completed runs
Below the metric cards, render a table of members. Each row shows:
Certificate status — green tick or amber pending badge
Risk level badge — colour coded from their avg finalRisk
Do NOT show individual scenario choices or personal decisions to managers — only aggregate scores. This protects employee privacy and builds trust in the platform.
9. Business Model — Pricing Pages & License Gating
A marketing page explaining the enterprise offering. Include:
Hero: "Replace boring PowerPoint HR training with AI simulations"
Industry selector cards (Corporate / Manufacturing / Healthcare / Banking)
CTA: "Request a Demo" → contact form → creates a lead record
// Check org license before allowing /org/* routes
// Fetch org from DB (use edge-compatible Prisma or a lightweight check)
Do not jump around. Each step builds on the previous one.
Add 4 industry scenarios to lib/scenarios.ts (1 per package)
Add remaining scenario content (4-6 per package)
Total estimated implementation time: ~25 hours of focused development. Steps 1–6 are the minimum viable enterprise version. Steps 7–12 complete the product.
If asked "How will industries use your product?" — say exactly this:

"SafePath is an AI-powered simulation training platform where employees practice real workplace scenarios — safety incidents, ethical dilemmas, compliance situations — before facing them in real life. Instead of boring PowerPoint training, they make decisions, get instant AI feedback, and earn verifiable compliance certificates."
Slide 1 — Show the manufacturing scenario: machine malfunction, three choices, Risk Meter updating in real time
Slide 2 — Show the AI coach panel: industry-specific feedback citing Factories Act 1948
Slide 3 — Switch to Manager Dashboard: completion rate, avg confidence, member table with certificate badges
Slide 4 — Show the compliance report download (CSV with name, dept, cert date)
Slide 5 — Show the enterprise pricing page and "Request Demo" CTA
"Why not just use LMS like Moodle?"
"LMS delivers content. SafePath makes employees practice decisions in real-time under pressure, with AI feedback. Outcome-based learning vs. content delivery."
"How is this better than video training?"
"Video is passive. Our simulations are branching — your choices change the outcome. Decision-making muscle memory is built by doing, not watching."
"How do you handle data privacy for employees?"
"Managers only see aggregate scores. Individual choices and session content are never exposed to employers."
"Pilot with 1 manufacturing company (10 seats, free 30-day trial). Convert to ₹300/seat/year. Upsell to full enterprise license at scale."
SafePath Enterprise Implementation Guide | Based on github.com/SmritiD2004/safepath | v1.0
